decision: Form I base verb; active past — 'created', not 'was created' (خُلِقَ).
ḍamma-kasra vowelling = passive; gloss must be passive even though the consonant skeleton equals خَلَقَ.
Form II (shadda on zāy) intensive; do NOT collapse with Form IV أَنزَلَ.
Form IV (hamza) causative; distinct verb from II نَزَّلَ and from Form I نَزَلَ 'descended'.
Form II causative of عَلِمَ 'knew'; 'taught' not 'knew'.
Form IV causative — 'brought forth/out', not Form I خَرَجَ 'went out'.
Form V reflexive; the maṣdar ذِكْر (Form I noun) = 'remembrance' takes a nominal gloss, not a verb.
Form VIII; first radical و assimilates into the infixed ت (و→ت). norm() must not seek a *وتق* stem.
Form VIII with ت→د assimilation after زاي; hollow root (ز-ي-د). Do not read a *زدد* root.
Form X requestative imperative — 'seek forgiveness', distinct from Form I غَفَرَ 'forgave'.
Form X estimative ('deem oneself great'); not Form I كَبُرَ 'was great'.
Form I maṣdar -> NOMINAL gloss. Never 'he remembered'. Homograph guard: ذَكَر 'male' is a different word (vowels).
Form IV maṣdar إِفْعَال pattern; nominal 'the act of spending', not a finite verb.
Active participle (agent noun) -> nominal; never the verb 'to disbelieve'.
Passive participle (patient noun) مَفْعُول pattern; 'that which is created', not the verb.
Doubled root (C2=C3=د) with idghām + shadda; the shadda hides the third radical from norm()-style matching.
Four-radical فَعْلَلَ verb; do not force a triliteral root.
Form IV of a hollow root (ق-و-م); the medial و is lost in conjugation. 'established', not 'stood'.
